title: coelogyne pumila var. pumila
intro: Coelogyne pumila var. pumila is a type of orchid species that belongs to the family Orchidaceae, commonly referred to as the miniature Coelogyne orchid due to its compact and delicate appearance. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine, and its medicinal properties are attributed to its bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been found to exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Coelogyne pumila var. pumila are thought to include its potential to reduce stress, improve cognitive function, and provide relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are believed to promote overall well-being and support the body's natural healing processes.
